spec_match.rationale: The page now matches the current draft: zoom lives on `CaptureController`, exposes `getSupportedZoomLevels()` and `zoomLevel`, and changes zoom only through `increaseZoomLevel()`, `decreaseZoomLevel()`, and `resetZoomLevel()` from trusted click/input handlers.

interactive.rationale: The demo includes start/stop capture, live API probes, step/reset buttons, a supported-level planner slider, pre-capture rejection, synthetic-event rejection, and an arbitrary-target branch.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index now describes the `CaptureController` step-method model and no longer promises a `track.setZoomLevel()` or arbitrary percentage API.

edge_cases.rationale: The page surfaces no-capture `getSupportedZoomLevels()` errors, missing API probes, untrusted async zoom rejection, and explains that unsupported arbitrary percentages are not part of the current API.

references.rationale: The concept includes the required `https://chromestatus.com/feature/5092615678066688` link and the W3C draft reference in `see also`.

design_system.rationale: Local styling uses shared variables, keeps button colors paired, and includes a visible token-based origin-trial banner.

summary: Zoom Coach now reflects the current Captured Surface Control zoom contract, adds the required references and origin-trial banner, probes the live API, and demonstrates no-capture, untrusted-event, and arbitrary-target edge cases.
